news.title:
MFs increased exposure to private banks in Feb; SBI saw reduced investments.
HDFC Bank received Rs 7,000 cr inflows, leading private sector banking investments.
news.summary:
MFs increased exposure to private banks in Feb; SBI saw reduced investments.
HDFC Bank received Rs 7,000 cr inflows, leading private sector banking investments.

news.title:
HDFC Bank surged 3%, leading Nifty 50 recovery; analysts maintain 45 'Buy' ratings.
ICICI Bank rebounded, hitting 52-week low; analysts project over 40% upside potential.
news.summary:
HDFC Bank surged 3%, leading Nifty 50 recovery; analysts maintain 45 'Buy' ratings.
ICICI Bank rebounded, hitting 52-week low; analysts project over 40% upside potential.

news.title:
Investec maintains Buy on ICICI Bank, sets target price ₹1,685, citing FY27 credit growth outlook.
Resilience amid geopolitical tensions and PSU competition keeps ICICI Bank a top pick.
news.summary:
Investec maintains Buy on ICICI Bank, sets target price ₹1,685, citing FY27 credit growth outlook.
Resilience amid geopolitical tensions and PSU competition keeps ICICI Bank a top pick.

news.title:
Valuation gap between PSU and private banks narrows as PSU banks show steady P/E ratios and outperform private peers.
PSU bank index gained 47.3% YoY vs 6.5% for private banks; SBI rose 8.7%, while HDFC Bank fell 18.6% Dec-Mar.
news.summary:
Valuation gap between PSU and private banks narrows as PSU banks show steady P/E ratios and outperform private peers.
PSU bank index gained 47.3% YoY vs 6.5% for private banks; SBI rose 8.7%, while HDFC Bank fell 18.6% Dec-Mar.

news.title:
ICICI Bank fell 4.76% to Rs.1,313.35, surpassing Sensex's 3% drop.
Decline driven by selling pressure, technical weakness, and mixed sentiment.
news.summary:
ICICI Bank fell 4.76% to Rs.1,313.35, surpassing Sensex's 3% drop.
Decline driven by selling pressure, technical weakness, and mixed sentiment.

news.title:
TCS and ICICI Bank exit $100B market cap club amid market selloff.
Indian equities lose ₹740B since July 2022 due to weak rupee, tensions.
news.summary:
TCS and ICICI Bank exit $100B market cap club amid market selloff.
Indian equities lose ₹740B since July 2022 due to weak rupee, tensions.

news.title:
ICICI Bank saw a block trade on NSE with 518,046 shares at Rs. 1255.4.
The transaction totaled Rs. 65.04 crores, highlighting significant investor activity.
news.summary:
ICICI Bank saw a block trade on NSE with 518,046 shares at Rs. 1255.4.
The transaction totaled Rs. 65.04 crores, highlighting significant investor activity.

news.title:
Axis Bank, ICICI Bank, and HDFC Bank dragged Nifty; Bank Nifty fell 1%. Sensex dropped 640 points, Nifty declined 176 points.
Private banks fell sharply; PSU banks saw limited losses. FIIs sold Rs 4,673 cr, while DIIs bought Rs 6,333 cr, aiding markets.
news.summary:
Axis Bank, ICICI Bank, and HDFC Bank dragged Nifty; Bank Nifty fell 1%. Sensex dropped 640 points, Nifty declined 176 points.
Private banks fell sharply; PSU banks saw limited losses. FIIs sold Rs 4,673 cr, while DIIs bought Rs 6,333 cr, aiding markets.

news.title:
CLSA maintains Outperform on ICICI Bank, targets Rs 1,700, citing retail loan growth (~7% YoY) & lower credit costs.
Stock trades at ~2× PB and 14× forward PE, indicating rerating potential.
news.summary:
CLSA maintains Outperform on ICICI Bank, targets Rs 1,700, citing retail loan growth (~7% YoY) & lower credit costs.
Stock trades at ~2× PB and 14× forward PE, indicating rerating potential.

news.title:
ICICI Bank shows a rounded double top with a break of ascending trendline.
Weekly close below horizontal support and increased selling volume observed.
news.summary:
ICICI Bank shows a rounded double top with a break of ascending trendline.
Weekly close below horizontal support and increased selling volume observed.

news.title:

The transaction totaled Rs. 33.41 crores on the NSE platform.
news.summary:

news.title:
SEBI approves manager change for 5 Category II AIFs managed by ICICI Venture Funds.
Portfolio transfer to ICICI Prudential AMC enables business reorganization for specific SEBI-registered AIFs.
news.summary:
SEBI approves manager change for 5 Category II AIFs managed by ICICI Venture Funds.

news.title:
ICICI Bank board approves buying 2% more in life insurance arm, aiming to retain majority stake.
Purchase subject to regulatory approvals; meeting held from Feb 26 to Feb 28.
news.summary:
ICICI Bank board approves buying 2% more in life insurance arm, aiming to retain majority stake.
Purchase subject to regulatory approvals; meeting held from Feb 26 to Feb 28.

news.title:
Sensex & Nifty posted 3rd worst weekly fall of 2026; Nifty IT fell 19% in February.
Bharti Airtel, ICICI Bank, HDFC Bank among key losers; Tata Motors rose 3% on CLSA rating.
news.summary:
Sensex & Nifty posted 3rd worst weekly fall of 2026; Nifty IT fell 19% in February.
Bharti Airtel, ICICI Bank, HDFC Bank among key losers; Tata Motors rose 3% on CLSA rating.

news.title:
Bank Nifty fell 500 points, down 0.8% at 60,717; ICICI Bank, HDFC Bank led losses.
PSU banks like SBI, BoB saw limited losses; India VIX rose 4%, signalling higher volatility.
news.summary:
Bank Nifty fell 500 points, down 0.8% at 60,717; ICICI Bank, HDFC Bank led losses.
PSU banks like SBI, BoB saw limited losses; India VIX rose 4%, signalling higher volatility.

news.title:
ICICI Bank, Bharti Airtel, and Reliance lead Nifty losses as markets decline.
Nearly 400 Nifty 500 stocks drop; FMCG, auto components sectors trade lower.
news.summary:
ICICI Bank, Bharti Airtel, and Reliance lead Nifty losses as markets decline.
Nearly 400 Nifty 500 stocks drop; FMCG, auto components sectors trade lower.

news.title:
Apple is in talks with ICICI Bank, HDFC Bank, and Axis Bank to launch Apple Pay in India by mid-2026.
Apple Pay aims to support UPI and card payments, competing with Google Pay, PhonePe, and Paytm in India's growing digital payments market.
news.summary:
Apple is in talks with ICICI Bank, HDFC Bank, and Axis Bank to launch Apple Pay in India by mid-2026.
Apple Pay aims to support UPI and card payments, competing with Google Pay, PhonePe, and Paytm in India's growing digital payments market.

news.title:
ICICI Bank sees increased put option activity, hinting at cautious investor sentiment ahead of March 2026 expiry.
Put contracts at Rs 1,400 strike price rise with higher open interest and turnover, indicating hedging strategies.
news.summary:
ICICI Bank sees increased put option activity, hinting at cautious investor sentiment ahead of March 2026 expiry.
Put contracts at Rs 1,400 strike price rise with higher open interest and turnover, indicating hedging strategies.

news.title:
Kotak Institutional sees ICICI, Axis, and SBI as strong picks amid PSU-private bank narrowing.
Mid-tier PSU banks show operating metrics akin to private lenders; growth at 13-15% with lower credit costs.
news.summary:
Kotak Institutional sees ICICI, Axis, and SBI as strong picks amid PSU-private bank narrowing.
Mid-tier PSU banks show operating metrics akin to private lenders; growth at 13-15% with lower credit costs.

news.title:
NPS Tier II schemes offer flexible investments with no lock-in, delivering up to 18.8% in equity, 8.6% in corporate bonds, and 9.1% in government bonds.
Top performers include Tata Pension Fund (18.8% equity), HDFC Pension Fund (8.6% corporate bonds), and LIC (9.1% government bonds).
news.summary:
NPS Tier II schemes offer flexible investments with no lock-in, delivering up to 18.8% in equity, 8.6% in corporate bonds, and 9.1% in government bonds.
Top performers include Tata Pension Fund (18.8% equity), HDFC Pension Fund (8.6% corporate bonds), and LIC (9.1% government bonds).
